description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a dark, almost black coloration on its sides, while the top surface is a lighter gray, suggesting a different finish or exposure. The edges are sharp and well-defined, indicating recent cutting or minimal weathering. The overall appearance is utilitarian, likely designed for durability and traction. Its robust construction suggests it is intended for use in high-traffic urban environments.
description: A rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on its top surface, alternating between dark and light gray. The block is solid, heavy, and designed for durability and traction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block with visible parallel grooves running horizontally.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically in this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the same striped groove pattern across the width.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or pedestrian crossing zone
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and edges are sharp, indicating recent installation.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with adjacent pavement or blocks
